mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, invalidating the threshold signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising inflation erode household purchasing power, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS falls and CPIAUCSL rises, with a rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic downturns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions do not predict negative 5-day returns during recessionary periods, with positive returns occurring in over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0% sustain corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 2.0% growth fail to correlate with positive 60-day returns, showing a rank IC below 0.01 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.4%, breaking the threshold mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shifts that weaken wage growth, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows no predictive power for negative returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signal a collapsing construction sector that drags down related equity sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during credit crunches.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1.0 million fail to predict negative 5-day returns during financial stress periods, with positive returns exceeding 55% of triggered instances.
